title: 3 Hong Kong AI Stocks to Buy Now If You Want to Invest Like Michael Burry
excerpt: Michael Burry is turning bullish on beaten-down Hong Kong AI stocks, JD.com, Alibaba, and Tencent, which could offer compelling long-term upside as the AI ra...
content_html:
Legendary investor Michael Burry, best known for predicting the 2008 financial crisis, recently suggested that now is "a particularly good time" to look for undervalued Hong Kong stocks as investors rotate away from expensive AI hardware names. His comments have renewed interest in China's internet giants, many of which are trading well below previous highs despite making aggressive investments in artificial intelligence (AI).

Among the most compelling opportunities are JD.com (JD), Alibaba Group Holding Limited (BABA), and Tencent Holdings Limited (TCEHY). Together, these companies rank among the largest technology constituents of the Hang Seng Index ($HSI) and are increasingly positioning themselves as long-term AI leaders rather than simply e-commerce or social media companies.

More News from Barchart

  • Huge, Unusual Intel Options Volume Today Ahead of Earnings This Week

  • Intel Stock Is Down, But Put Premiums are High - Put Short Sellers Love the High Yields

  • The Number Tesla Stock Bulls Are Really Waiting for This Earnings Season Has Nothing to Do With Cars

  • Tired of missing midday reversals? The FREE Barchart Brief newsletter keeps you in the know. Sign up now!

He recently increased his stake in JD.com and argues that investors should look to Hong Kong as enthusiasm for markets like South Korea, Japan, and semiconductor stocks begins to fade. However, despite growing optimism from firms like Morgan Stanley (MS), the Hang Seng Index has declined this year, with major Chinese technology companies also posting year-to-date (YTD) losses. Given this backdrop, the above-mentioned stocks could be prominent additions to unlock solid gains in the long term. 

Stock #1: JD.com

Based in Beijing, China, JD.com, Inc. is one of the country's largest supply chain-based technology and e-commerce companies, operating a nationwide logistics network while offering retail, logistics, healthcare, and technology services. The company has a market cap of $38.2 billion.

JD shares have declined 8.9% over the past 52 weeks but up 6.1% YTD, compared to the Hang Seng Index, which has increased marginally 0.55% over the past year but is down 1.94% this year. Also, in comparison, the S&P 500 Index ($SPX) delivered  19.1% returns over the past year and 9.7% gains this year.

The recent weakness has caused the stock to trade at a discount. The stock trades at 10.65 times price-to-earnings, which is below the sector median and its own five-year average.

JD.com reported its first-quarter 2026 results on May 12, with revenue rising 4.9% year-over-year (YOY) to RMB315.7 billion ($45.8 billion). Growth was driven by a 20.6% YOY increase in net service revenue, including a 21.7% rise in logistics and other service revenue and an 18.8% increase in marketplace and marketing revenue, highlighting the continued expansion of JD's higher-margin businesses. General merchandise revenue climbed 14.9% YOY, while annual active customers reached a record high.

General merchandise revenue climbed 14.9% YOY, while annual active customers reached a record high.

sentences.en: JD.com reported its first-quarter 2026 results on May 12, with revenue rising 4.9% year-over-year (YOY) to RMB315.7 billion ($45.8 billion).
sentences.zh: 京东于 5 月 12 日公布了 2026 年第一季度业绩，收入同比增长 4.9% 至 3,157 亿元人民币（458 亿美元）。

sentences.en: Growth was driven by a 20.6% YOY increase in net service revenue, including a 21.7% rise in logistics and other service revenue and an 18.8% increase in marketplace and marketing revenue, highlighting the continued expansion of JD's higher-margin businesses.
sentences.zh: 增长的推动因素是净服务收入同比增长 20.6%，其中物流和其他服务收入增长 21.7%，市场和营销收入增长 18.8%，凸显京东高利润业务的持续扩张。

sentences.en: General merchandise revenue climbed 14.9% YOY, while annual active customers reached a record high.
sentences.zh: 百货收入同比增长14.9%，年度活跃客户创历史新高。

content_html_zh:
以预测 2008 年金融危机而闻名的传奇投资者迈克尔·伯里 (Michael Burry) 最近表示，随着投资者放弃昂贵的人工智能硬件品牌，现在是寻找被低估的香港股票的“特别好时机”。 他的言论重新燃起了人们对中国互联网巨头的兴趣，尽管在人工智能（AI）领域进行了积极投资，但其中许多互联网巨头的股价仍远低于之前的高点。

最引人注目的机会包括京东（JD）、阿里巴巴集团控股有限公司（BABA）和腾讯控股有限公司（TCEHY）。 这些公司共同跻身恒生指数（HSI）最大的科技成分股之列，并且越来越多地将自己定位为长期人工智能领导者，而不仅仅是电子商务或社交媒体公司。

来自条形图的更多新闻

  • 在本周财报发布之前，今天英特尔期权交易量异常巨大
  • 英特尔股价下跌，但看跌期权溢价很高——看跌期权卖空者喜欢高收益
  • 特斯拉股票多头真正等待的财报季数量与汽车无关
  • 厌倦了错过午间反转吗？ 免费的条形图简讯让您了解最新情况。 立即注册！

他最近增持了京东的股份，并认为投资者应该关注香港，因为对韩国、日本和半导体股票等市场的热情开始消退。 然而，尽管摩根士丹利 (MS) 等公司的乐观情绪日益增强，但恒生指数今年仍出现下跌，中国主要科技公司也公布了年初至今 (YTD) 的亏损。 在这种背景下，上述股票可能会成为重要的补充，以释放长期的稳健收益。

股票#1：京东

京东公司总部位于中国北京，是中国最大的供应链技术和电子商务公司之一，运营着覆盖全国的物流网络，同时提供零售、物流、医疗保健和技术服务。 该公司市值为 382 亿美元。

京东股价在过去 52 周内下跌 8.9%，但年初至今上涨 6.1%，而恒生指数在过去一年小幅上涨 0.55%，但今年下跌 1.94%。 此外，相比之下，标准普尔 500 指数 ($SPX) 去年的回报率为 19.1%，今年的回报率为 9.7%。

最近的疲软导致该股以折扣价交易。 该股的市盈率为 10.65 倍，低于行业中位数及其五年平均水平。

京东于 5 月 12 日公布了 2026 年第一季度业绩，收入同比增长 4.9% 至 3,157 亿元人民币（458 亿美元）。 增长的推动因素是净服务收入同比增长 20.6%，其中物流和其他服务收入增长 21.7%，市场和营销收入增长 18.8%，凸显京东高利润业务的持续扩张。 百货收入同比增长14.9%，年度活跃客户创历史新高。
